content:
Expose read-only state. Keep control of mutations inside the feature that owns them.

3 min read
6 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Signals Make State Easy
Angular Signals have made state management much simpler.
Creating state is straightforward.
users = signal<User[]>([]);Updating it is equally simple.
this.users.update(users => [
...users,
newUser
]);The problem isn’t Signals.
The problem is exposing writable Signals to the rest of the application.
The Hidden Risk
Imagine a feature service like this:
@Injectable()
export class CustomerStore {
readonly customers = signal<Customer[]>([]);
}Another component can now do this:
this.customerStore.customers.set([]);Or even:
this.customerStore.customers.update(() => []);Every consumer now has permission to change your feature state.
The owner of the state has lost control.
State Should Have One Owner
